quotes.text: The secret ingredient in my grandmother's tiramisu recipe is cardamom, which absolutely horrifies food historians. I'm keeping it secret from them out of pure spite.
quotes.speaker: Lidia Bastianich
quotes.fake_note: Lidia is known for both honoring traditional Italian cooking and playfully subverting expectations. An invented unconventional ingredient paired with dry humor about food gatekeeping feels authentically hers.

quotes.text: When I was designing the original iPod, I spent three weeks arguing with someone about whether the scroll wheel should click. It did not click. I was right, but I have never been more certain I was wrong.
quotes.speaker: Jonathan Ive
quotes.fake_note: Ive is famous for obsessing over tiny design details and second-guessing himself publicly. The quote captures his verbose, paradoxical way of discussing decisions without claiming anything false about his work.

quotes.text: I once tried to explain to my kids what a DVD is. They thought I was describing a religion. Honestly, they might be right.
quotes.speaker: Bill Burr
quotes.fake_note: Classic Burr structure: bewilderment at technology becoming obsolete, landing in a self-aware absurdist punchline. Totally in his wheelhouse of generational exasperation.

quotes.text: What would take Hubble 2,000 years to process, Roman can do in a year.
quotes.speaker: Jared Isaacman
quotes.context: NASA Administrator, on the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope

quotes.text: I don't know if those are B's. They might be Cardi D's. I'm pretty sure those aren't B's. She's got the wrong initials.
quotes.speaker: Charles Barkley
quotes.context: on the TNT desk during Cardi B's NBA Finals halftime show

quotes.text: I've always said that tennis is 90% mental, 10% physical, and 5% just accepting that your strings will betray you at the worst possible moment.
quotes.speaker: Serena Williams
quotes.fake_note: The math is deliberately broken (105% total), which is the kind of self-aware joke Serena might make about sports clichés. The specific frustration about strings is absurdly relatable for any player.
